name: George Matthews
abstract: George Matthews (September 23, 1912 – June 28, 1982) was a jazz trombonist.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Dizzy Gillespie: Genius at Midnight
Newport Jazz Festival: Salute to Ella Fitzgerald
New York Jazz Repertory Company: The Savoy Ballroom "The Home of Happy Feet"

works.t: When the Lights Go On Again (All Over the World)

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: It was Chu, Hot Lips Paige, George Matthews on Trombone, Buster Bailey, Horace Henderson, Lawrence Lucie, Israel Crosby and you, and that was in 1937.

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: Big George Matthews was a trombone player. He had been a member of the Count Basie band... He had a very, very unique style.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Quentin Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: Oh, big George Matthews was one of the trombone players, I think, in there, too, for a while.

about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Green
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Oh. Ted Donelly, George Matthews, Eli Robinson. [INTERVIEWEE] Mm hmm.
